title: Smoky Savory Brazilian Chicken Burger
description: Umami, juicy, and packed with flavors reminiscent of Brazil, this chicken burger is perfect for brunch or a weekend lunch.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine ground chicken breast, chopped celery, onion, garlic, paprika, salt, black pepper, and Brazilian-style cure. Mix well with your hands until just combined.
Divide the chicken mixture into 4 equal portions and shape each into a ball. Flatten each ball slightly into a patty.
Brush the grill grates with olive oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the chicken patties for 5-6 minutes per side, or until cooked through to an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).
While the chicken is cooking, toast the brioche buns on the grill.
Assemble the burgers by spreading a slice of avocado on the bottom bun, followed by a cooked chicken patty, a slice of red bell pepper, and finally the top bun.
If desired, serve with additional toppings such as sliced tomatoes, shredded lettuce, or a dollop of spicy mayo.
Leftover burgers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat by grilling or pan-frying until warmed through.
